data.title: Harvard Adjusts Investment in BlackRock's Crypto Trusts
data.description: Crypto Rover posted on X that Harvard University has made significant changes to its investment portfolio concerning BlackRock's cryptocurrency trusts. The university reduced its holdings in the BlackRock iShares Bitcoin Trust by approximately 21%, bringing the total to $265.8 million. Simultaneously, Harvard initiated a new investment in BlackRock's iShares Ethereum Trust, valued between $86.8 million and $87 million, during the fourth quarter of 2025.

data.title: New Wallet’s SIREN Withdrawal Shows $9M Unrealized Gain in 10 Days
data.description: A newly created wallet that withdrew a large amount of SIREN from a centralized exchange is now sitting on a sizable unrealized profit, according to on-chain data.Blockchain monitoring service Onchain Lens reported that the wallet withdrew 71.84 million SIREN from a CEX 10 days ago. Based on current market prices, the position is now showing an unrealized profit of approximately $8.98 million.

data.title: Crypto Fear and Greed Index Rises to 12 but Remains in ‘Extreme Fear’
data.description: Crypto market sentiment showed a marginal improvement but remains deeply pessimistic, with the Fear and Greed Index ticking up to 12, still firmly in the “Extreme Fear” zone, according to data from Alternative.

data.title: Hong Kong's Cheng Family to Compensate Giordano Shareholders After Investigation
data.description: Hong Kong's Cheng family has agreed to pay up to HK$1.5 billion to shareholders of Giordano International, a clothing company, following an investigation by the city. Bloomberg posted on X, detailing the financial settlement reached after scrutiny of the company's operations. The agreement aims to resolve issues raised during the investigation, ensuring fair compensation for the shareholders involved. This development marks a significant financial move by the Cheng family, reflecting their commitment to addressing the concerns raised by the authorities. The settlement is expected to have implications for the company's future operations and shareholder relations.

data.title: Whale Opens Leveraged BTC and ETH Short Positions
data.description: A whale has reportedly opened a 20x leveraged short position on Bitcoin, amounting to 50 BTC, and increased its Ethereum short position to 21,000 ETH. According to PANews, the current unrealized profit from these positions stands at $1.47 million.

data.title: Charles Schwab Seeks Product Manager for Digital Assets and Stablecoins
data.description:
Charles Schwab is recruiting a product manager for its digital assets and stablecoins division. According to Foresight News, the role involves enhancing and expanding stablecoin product features to improve customer experience. The position aligns with Schwab's strategy of prioritizing customer perspectives while optimizing revenue, risk management, and overall product value.

Candidates are required to have over five years of experience in retail online brokerage or the cryptocurrency industry, focusing on product, strategy, or operations. A proven track record in developing or supporting digital asset product experiences related to stablecoins is also necessary.

data.title: Whale '0x15a4' Opens High-Leverage Bitcoin Short
data.description: Onchain Lens posted on X that a cryptocurrency whale known as '0x15a4' has initiated a short position on Bitcoin with 20x leverage. Additionally, the whale has expanded their short position in Ethereum, currently holding an unrealized profit of $1.47 million. The whale's positions include 21,000 ETH, valued at $41.58 million, and 50 BTC, valued at $3.43 million.

data.title: Ethereum's Performance Surprises Investors in Current Cycle
data.description: Crypto Rover posted on X. This cycle, Ethereum ($ETH) has surprised many investors with its underperformance. Looking ahead, there is speculation that in the next cycle, Ethereum may exceed expectations with its performance.

data.title: India's National Highways Authority Plans IPO for Infrastructure Trust
data.description: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) is reportedly gearing up to launch an initial public offering (IPO) for its infrastructure investment trust as early as next month. Bloomberg posted on X, citing unnamed sources familiar with the matter. The move is part of NHAI's strategy to raise funds for its ongoing and future infrastructure projects. The IPO is expected to attract significant interest from investors looking to capitalize on India's expanding infrastructure sector. Details regarding the size and valuation of the offering have not been disclosed. The NHAI's decision to proceed with the IPO underscores the growing trend of infrastructure investment trusts in India, which aim to provide a steady income stream for investors while supporting the country's infrastructure development goals.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Trump Highlights Corporate Citizenship Concerns in America
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has brought attention to a significant issue regarding American corporations, emphasizing their diminishing role as responsible citizens. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these companies are increasingly prioritizing profits over social responsibility. This shift has raised concerns about the ethical practices of businesses and their impact on society.

The focus on profit maximization has led to a decline in corporate accountability, with companies often neglecting their duties to the communities they operate in. This trend has sparked debates about the need for businesses to balance financial success with social obligations.

Critics argue that the lack of corporate citizenship is detrimental to societal progress, as it undermines efforts to address social issues and promote sustainable development. The discussion around this topic continues to evolve, with calls for companies to adopt more ethical practices and contribute positively to society.

data.title: Deutsche Bank Questions US Dollar's Safe-Haven Status Amid AI Investment Risks
data.description: Deutsche Bank's recent analysis raises doubts about the US dollar's role as a dependable safe-haven asset. According to NS3.AI, the bank points to a diminishing correlation between the dollar and equities, suggesting a shift in its traditional safe-haven status. The report highlights the risks associated with significant AI investments by major tech firms, which have led to increased market volatility and uncertain returns in the US. Furthermore, the trend of foreign investors hedging their dollar exposure indicates a waning confidence in the dollar as a reliable portfolio hedge.

data.title: A whale deposited 3.16 million USDC into HyperLiquid and opened a limit buy order for XMR.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited 3.16 million USDC into HyperLiquid and placed limit buy orders for XMR in the price range of $250-$315. Since January 15, the whale has purchased 7,189 XMR, worth approximately $3.59 million. These tokens have now been distributed to multiple wallets.

data.title: Bitcoin’s Quiet Buyers Absorb Record Supply During Market Dip
data.description:
Key Takeaways Accumulator addresses are buying at record levels, averaging about 372,000 BTC per month.
source: https://coindoo.com/bitcoins-quiet-buyers-absorb-record-supply-during-market-dip/

data.title: Hong Kong Pioneers Digital Yuan in Asset Settlement
data.description: Wang Yang, Vice President of the University of Hong Kong, announced that the city has begun experimenting with the digital yuan in its digital asset market settlement processes. According to NS3.AI, Hong Kong is also leading the issuance of digital bonds settled with both the digital yuan and the digital Hong Kong dollar. This initiative positions Hong Kong as a key player in mainland China's real-world asset markets and suggests the potential rise of offshore renminbi stablecoins supported by digital yuan reserves.

data.title: Logan Paul's Pikachu Illustrator Card Sets Auction Record
data.description:
A Pikachu Illustrator card owned by U.S. influencer Logan Paul has set a new auction record, selling for $16,492,000 at Goldin Auctions on February 16. According to BlockBeats, this card, considered the 'Holy Grail' of Pokémon collectibles, achieved a hammer price of $13.3 million, with an additional 24% buyer's premium bringing the total to over three times the amount Paul paid in 2021.

The card, which originated from a 1998 Pokémon illustration contest in Japan's CoroCoro Comic magazine, is one of only 39-41 ever awarded and was never publicly sold. It was hand-drawn by Pikachu's original designer, Atsuko Nishida, and features the exclusive 'Illustrator' designation. Logan Paul's card is the only known PSA 10 graded example, earning it the nickname 'Mona Lisa of the Pokémon world.'

The auction lasted 41 days and saw intense bidding, culminating in 97 bids during an extended bidding phase. Logan Paul attended the event, delivering the card along with a diamond-encrusted necklace he wore at WrestleMania 38 to the new owner, reportedly AJ Scaramucci, founder of Solari Capital and son of former White House Communications Director Anthony Scaramucci.

data.title: India's Trade Deficit Expands Ahead of U.S. Tariff Agreement
data.description: India's trade deficit saw an increase in January, occurring shortly before New Delhi reached a temporary agreement with the United States to reduce tariffs. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the timing of this economic development as significant for both nations. The interim pact aims to ease trade tensions and foster better economic relations between the two countries. This agreement is expected to have implications for various sectors, potentially influencing future trade dynamics. The widening deficit underscores the challenges India faces in balancing its trade relationships while pursuing economic growth.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Trump Offers Support to Hungary Amid Financial Concerns
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is prepared to offer assistance to Hungary should it face financial difficulties, according to Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Bloomberg posted on X that Rubio made the statement during a joint press conference with Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban in Budapest. The announcement underscores the ongoing diplomatic relations between the United States and Hungary, highlighting potential economic collaboration. The discussion took place amid broader geopolitical considerations, with both nations expressing a commitment to strengthening ties and addressing mutual concerns.

data.title: Dogecoin's Price Rebound Amid Anticipation of X Money Integration
data.description: Discussions surrounding Dogecoin (DOGE) have intensified due to expectations of its integration into X Money, a new payment feature by Elon Musk's social platform X. According to NS3.AI, DOGE's price has experienced a short-term rebound, rising from $0.09 to over $0.11. Analysts are now targeting a move towards the 200-day moving average, estimated to be around $0.16–$0.17. Despite this price movement, institutional demand remains weak, as indicated by stagnant DOGE Spot ETF inflows. Additionally, the token's unlimited supply continues to challenge its long-term value retention.

data.title: Shiba Inu Price Hits Yearly Low Amid Lack of Token Burns
data.description: Shiba Inu (SHIB) experienced a significant price drop to $0.0000056 during the recent Valentine's Day weekend, marking its lowest point this year. According to NS3.AI, the absence of token burns during this period has contributed to the decline, as the burn rate has remained consistently low over the past year. This lack of token scarcity is affecting demand and raising concerns among investors about SHIB's potential for upward price movement. With anticipated network activity on Shibarium still weak, questions are emerging about SHIB's ability to achieve milestones such as reaching $0.01 without substantial token burns.
